title: Hodge via Langlands Universality — LUC-30 as Depth Forcing

analysis.object: T743: Hodge via Langlands Universality — LUC-30 as Depth Forcing depth analysis

luc_universality.description: LUC: 36 confirmed instances, all equate EML-2 and EML-3 shadows
luc_universality.reason: Universal pattern -- every natural duality is {EML-2, EML-3}

hodge_as_luc30.description: Hodge = LUC-30: duality between algebraic (EML-0/EML-2) and cohomological (EML-3)

langlands_forcing.description: LUC universality: if 35 instances work, the 36th (Hodge) should too

universality_mechanism.description: Why does LUC hold universally? The two-level ring {EML-2,EML-3} is closed (T254)
universality_mechanism.reason: Closed ring: operations on {EML-2,EML-3} stay in {EML-2,EML-3}

hodge_in_ring.description: Hodge conjecture = claim that cycle class map closes the {EML-2,EML-3} ring at Hodge level

forcing_argument.description: If {EML-2,EML-3} ring is closed for all 35 LUC instances, closure forces Hodge

t743_result.description: LUC universality + closed ring = strong structural forcing toward Hodge surjectivity -- T743
t743_result.reason: Conditional: if LUC is truly universal (no exceptions), Hodge follows

analysis.theorem: T743: Hodge via Langlands Universality — LUC-30 as Depth Forcing (S1022).

key_theorem: T743: Hodge via Langlands Universality — LUC-30 as Depth Forcing (S1022).
